articles.title: Most AI agents never reach production. Agent Bricks by Databricks
articles.summary: fixes that by helping you build agents that actually work — accurate, reliable and grounded in your data.

articles.title: See why it's worth your time
A BUSINESS THAT SCALES WITH THE VALUE OF INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: OpenAI believes that its business model should scale with the value intelligence delivers. It introduced consumer subscriptions as people demanded more capability and reliability, created workplace subscriptions, and added usage-based pricing so costs scale with real work getting done. It has more recently applied the principle to commerce, helping people choose what to purchase next and explore products. Advertising follows the same arc.

articles.title: ADS IN CHATGPT
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to introduce ads in the free and ChatGPT Go tiers to support broader access to AI. Ads will be tested first in the US, while ensuring user trust, data privacy, and ad quality.

articles.title: ELON MUSK'S XAI BRINGS 1GW COLOSSUS 2 AI TRAINING CLUSTER ONLINE
articles.summary: xAI's Colossus 2 supercomputer is now online. It is the first gigawatt-scale AI training cluster in the world, with upgrades still to come. xAI has been building infrastructure at a speed that far exceeds its primary rivals. The company's Colossus 1 and 2 systems now represent more than one million H100 GPU equivalents.

articles.title: BUGBOT'S CODE REVIEW EVOLUTION
articles.summary: Cursor's Bugbot has evolved into a capable AI code reviewer that detects logic, performance, and security issues in pull requests.

articles.title: THE BITTER LESSON OF AGENT FRAMEWORKS
articles.summary: An agent is just a for-loop of messages. Agent frameworks aren't required. Models only need a complete action space, a for-loop, an explicit exit, and context management. Every abstraction is a liability, and every 'helper' is a failure point. The less you build, the more it works.

articles.title: ON THE COMING INDUSTRIALISATION OF EXPLOIT GENERATION WITH LLMS
articles.summary: Agents built on GPT-5.2 and Opus 4.5 autonomously generated over 40 distinct working exploits for a zero-day QuickJS vulnerability across multiple scenarios, solving complex exploitation tasks in under an hour and with modest token costs. The experiment suggests exploit development will soon be limited more by token throughput than human hacker expertise, meaning offensive cybersecurity tasks could scale like industrial processes. This shift implies both unprecedented threat automation and urgency for preparing defense strategies as LLMs move beyond code assistance into full exploit synthesis.

articles.title: FULLY REMOTE)
articles.summary: We're hiring the first engineer on TLDR's new Applied AI team. You'll build production AI agents and design modular, composable Claude Skills to let non-technical teammates create their own AI workflows. Learn more [12].

articles.title: BENCHMARKING AI AGENT MEMORY: IS A FILESYSTEM ALL YOU NEED?
articles.summary: An agent's memory depends on its architecture, tools, and the underlying model. You can always mix and match frameworks, tools, and models. Simple file system tools are sufficient for a well-designed agent. More complex memory tools can be plugged in via MCP or custom tools.

articles.title: UNIQUENESS-AWARE RL FOR LLM DIVERSITY
articles.summary: MIT researchers have proposed a new reinforcement learning method that rewards diverse high-level solution strategies using LLM-based clustering of rollouts. This method improves pass@k across tasks without degrading pass@1.

articles.title: SCALING TRILLION-PARAMETER MODELS WITH PP
articles.summary: SGLang's Chunked Pipeline Parallelism enables efficient ultra-long context inference at scale. By combining dynamic chunking with asynchronous communication, its PP4 TP8 setup achieved higher throughput on large clusters.

articles.title: CHUNKHOUND
articles.summary: chunkhound is a Rust-based AI chunking library that splits long documents into semantically meaningful chunks for embeddings and retrieval-augmented workflows.

articles.title: THE A IN AGI STANDS FOR ADS
articles.summary: OpenAI is not dying or running out of money. While it has created a crazy circular economy, it's unlikely that it is looking to be acquired. Ads are the key to unlocking profitability. This article attempts to model OpenAI's expected annual revenue per user by understanding what it is actually building and how it compares to existing ad platforms.

articles.title: THE CODE-ONLY AGENT
articles.summary: Building an agent can be overwhelming. The ecosystem pushes developers toward complexity and the 'right' way to do things. This post explores a prompting pattern where agents create and run code as a response to every prompt. In this framework, agents can't do anything productive without writing code. This method produces a code witness of an answer, codifying the work in a very literal sense. It forces agents to produce proofs, run proofs, and interpret proof results.

articles.title: ULTRATHINK IS DEAD. LONG LIVE EXTENDED THINKING
INTRODUCING CHATGPT GO, NOW AVAILABLE WORLDWIDE
articles.summary: ChatGPT Go, launched initially in India in 2025, is now available globally as the most affordable AI subscription at $8/month in the US.

articles.title: $1 TRILLION UNLOCK FOR CONSUMER AI
articles.summary: AI agents that act became mainstream in 2025, but most users still can't provide the context needed to extract full value, creating a ceiling for reactive AI.
